mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly self-hosted AI platform designed for seamless integration with various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It is ideal for users looking to deploy AI solutions offline with built-in features for extensibility and user management. Key trade-offs include the need for local resource management and potential complexity in initial setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage your deployments easily.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

```

> This command downloads the CLI for Linux.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the desired version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster details.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> Install Open WebUI in the specified namespace.
commands: helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove the Open WebUI deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the application.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Backup current configuration.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Review the deployment configuration and logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or the port is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the service status and ensure the correct port is exposed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved user interface, and enhanced security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n open-webui'. All pods should be in the 'Running' state.

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
